programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Saw Too Much
programme.desc: At 106, Boris Pahor is the oldest living survivor of a Nazi concentration camp. He discusses life and death at Natzweiler, where almost half the prisoners died. Alan Yentob visits Pahor at his home in Trieste, where he talks about his fight against fascism and the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: The work of nine mountain vets, several nurses and stable hands in three very different veterinary practices located within the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland, as they care for a diverse range of animals in clinics, on farms and smallholdings. The Mourne area is well known for its stunning natural beauty and traditional country life, and the trio of practices respond to over 100 clinic cases and call-outs a day. In this episode, the founder of the oldest vet practice in Downpatrick, Maurice King, receives a call about an extraordinarily rare birth on a farm he knows well(n)

programme.title: Half a Sixpence (1967)
programme.desc: A humble draper's assistant in Edwardian London unexpectedly inherits a fortune from his grandfather. In his newly minted circumstances, he starts to drift away from his old friends in favour of the company of high-society swells, and upset his girlfriend when his head is turned by a lady from the upper classes. Musical based on HG Wells' Kipps, starring Tommy Steele, Julia Foster, Cyril Ritchard and Penelope Horner(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Memorable moments and previously unseen finds, including a handkerchief commemorating cricket legend Dr WG Grace and three postcard albums found on a council tip(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off. Plus, in-depth analysis of the weekend's performances(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former Love Island stars Wes Nelson and Georgia Steel join experts Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell in North Yorkshire on a quest for items to sell at an auction in North Shields. On a break from antique hunting, Wes takes Izzie to Scarborough Spa to hear about the resort's remarkable history, while the other pair go for a trip on a very old vessel(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Journeymen and the Junipers return for one last chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Malcolm I of Scotland, John Bradman, Kiefer Sutherland, and Ivanka Trump(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: Oskar is called away from Max and Clara's engagement party to investigate the brutal murders of three women in a brothel. Clara understands Max's eagerness to join the investigation and allows him to follow, much to the detective's dismay. The police almost immediately arrest Victor Krull, a man who regularly visited the brothel and took a particular interest in one of the victims, whose body has been washed and ritualistically displayed. The press are informed of the arrest, but Max is not at all convinced(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Bollywood: The World's Biggest Film Industry
programme.desc: Anita Rani concludes her exploration the world of Indian cinema by examining how a new generation of Bollywood stars are challenging the industry to keep up with the country's unusually young population - more than half the 1.3 billion people in India are under 25. She also investigates why Indian cinema is so fascinated with having westerners on screen, and visits one of Asia's largest slums to find out about an increasing trend in Bollywood to use the enormous power of cinema to bring about social change(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the third week of the competition, the candidates join Lord Sugar at London's Cambridge Theatre, where they find out the next task will challenge them to design a toy for six-to-eight-year-olds, along with an online video to promote their brand. The teams must create their toys and prepare their pitches, before presenting their concepts to some of the biggest names in the toy industry. Back in the boardroom, one candidate from the losing team will be sent home(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to the Balcaskie Estate in the East Neuk of Fife, where Charlotte Smith tries her hand at competitive ploughing, Ellie Harrison meets some local food producers, and Steve Brown is at Pittenweem harbour as the fishing boats bring in their catches. Meanwhile, it's a tense time for Adam Henson down on the farm as his cattle are tested for TB(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: As the Channel Tunnel's 25th anniversary collides with Brexit, meet the teams behind the scenes and beneath the seabed who keep everything on track through the longest undersea tunnel in the world(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Teenager Mia arrives at a summer camp on a remote island expecting to be Queen Bee, but is challenged by the mysterious Amber. Thriller, starring Lisa Ambalavanar(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: As her allies start to fall, Mia goes to face Amber head on, believing her own surrender will ensure their escape. But her rival has one last surprise(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: The ballot opens for Midsummer Queen, and Mia spots an opportunity to dethrone Amber by forming a new alliance. Out in the woods, friendship blossoms for Alex(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass return to tackle more potential miscarriages of justice as they re-examine historic murder cases on behalf of the family of those convicted. In the first edition they investigate their oldest case yet, the murder of a canal boat passenger 180 years ago in which boatmen were convicted and publicly hanged(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team take on a mid-century coffee table, a wind-up panda that holds special family memories and an unusual kitchen utensil in the form of a coconut scraper(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure aboard the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway, from Adelaide to Perth. Along the way he hears the story of an enterprising 19th-century Briton with a murky past who made his money growing vines, strikes it lucky in one of the most lucrative goldfields in the world and pays a flying visit to the doctor to hear how patients in remote Western Australia are cared for. He also hears how Edward VIII was caught up in a train accident near Bridgetown and learns about a child migration programme that cast a dark shadow on the history of both the UK and Australia(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Having reigned over musical theatre for nearly five decades, Andrew Lloyd Webber's music has delighted millions worldwide in hit shows like Jesus Christ Superstar, Evita, Cats and Aspects of Love. In this programme, the composer chats to Alan Yentob about his autobiography Unmasked, a candid and confessional account of his early life and career up to the opening of The Phantom of the Opera in 1986(n)

programme.title: Carmen Jones (1954)
programme.desc: A sultry female military worker attracts admiring glances from her male colleagues, but she has her eye on an engaged pilot. Otto Preminger's romantic musical drama, based on Bizet's opera Carmen with modernised lyrics and set in the Deep South during the Second World War. Dorothy Dandridge, Harry Belafonte, Olga James and Pearl Bailey star(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Christina values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the pair who avoided elimination in Sunday night's dance-off, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to on Saturday in Tunes on Tuesday(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The crew of the small fishing village of Howth respond to an emergency call from a family on a local beach, where a teenage girl on a sailing dinghy has capsized and is now being swept out into the Irish Sea. In Brighton, the crew are called out to what they think is just a routine job - towing a yacht that has lost power back to harbour - but a sudden spring squall and mountainous waves leave them fighting to save the yacht and themselves from being dashed on the shore(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the final week of the heats. For the Skills Test, Monica Galetti challenges three chefs to make a braised artichoke with saffron aquafaba mayonnaise, and Marcus Wareing sets three more the task of making chicken Schnitzel Holstein. All six contenders go through to the Signature round, which will determine who wins a place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Jessica Fostekew and Stephen Bailey(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 12th round of fixtures, including Baltimore Ravens v Los Angeles Rams(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste are on the trail of criminals extorting money from unsuspecting victims from online dating scams, beginning with a man who trick a businesswoman out of a total of almost £60,000 over the course of a few months(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: With up to 400 trains a day running on its 62 miles of track, the Channel Tunnel is the busiest railway system in the world. And doing things at the double is key to its success. Two countries built it. There are two terminals. And there is duplication at both ends. This episode reveals how teams of two work on both sides of the Channel to keep tourists and truckers moving. But how will preparations for Brexit affect such a well-oiled system of twinning and duplication, finely tuned over 25 years?(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: As the Midsummer Party rages on, things take a dark turn. Sparks fly, tears are spilled and more than one heart is broken before the night is through(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Alex and Petal delve deeper into the island and uncover a new mystery. Mia attempts to move on from her feud with Amber only to learn just how powerful her rival truly is(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Harry faces a steep challenge, but gets support from Petal. Mia and Alex reach out for help and end up getting a message from an unlikely source(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the mysterious murder of a Yorkshire farm owner in 1933, finding out if a clandestine love affair may have led to a brutal killing(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a wooden desk, a scrapbook charting the fantastic story of the owner's grandmother, and a wind up train that has not run in decades(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Fred Astaire
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing judge and former ballet star Darcey Bussell always wishes she could have danced with Fred Astaire, who died 30 years ago this year. Now she finds out all about him, on a journey that takes her from Nebraska to New York and from London to Los Angeles. Darcey discovers how Fred only started dancing to help his sister Adele, she learns about his discipline and inventiveness, and of course his Hollywood partnership with Ginger Rogers(n)

programme.title: Daddy Long Legs (1955)
programme.desc: An 18-year-old Frenchwoman falls in love with an older man, unaware he is the secret millionaire benefactor who saved her from a Paris orphanage and sponsored her education in America. Romantic musical, with Fred Astaire, Leslie Caron and Fred Clark(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women panellists Andrea McLean and Penny Lancaster search the antiques shops of Essex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from auctioneer Tim Medhust and dealer David Harper. Penny is already a keen collector and even finds the time to pick up a gift for husband Rod Stewart. How will newbie Andrea compare against her more experienced colleague?(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last heat of the competition, the first three chefs recreate Monica's dish of oeufs meurette, before the next three take on Marcus' drop scones and a suzette sauce. In the next round, the six chefs are given a chance to showcase their creativity and cooking style as they present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. The opening episode introduces the first set of the six expectant families featured in the series, including young first-time parents Syler and Mo Nicholson-Bayoumi from Nottinghamshire, and Helen and Nigel Pierce, who are expecting their fourth child(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the ninth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The vitamin industry is worth more than £400million a year in the UK alone, but what are these pills actually doing for people's health? Dr Giles Yeo aims to find out, exploring if vitamin and mineral supplements are what they appear to be and putting people's diets to the test to find out if they really need these supplements. Those he meets include a scientist in Copenhagen, whose groundbreaking research into antioxidants indicates they may not have the extraordinary health-enhancing properties that people believe, and a nutrition scientist from the University of Greenwich, who tells Giles that on average most people in the UK are meeting their vitamin requirements thorough their diet(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore describes how the Habsburgs transformed Vienna into a multi-national city of music, culture, art and ideas. Simon explores the Habsburgs' rise to power and discovers more about how Vienna marked Europe's front line in the struggle to defend both Christendom and the Catholic Church from revolutionaries who plotted to destroy them(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Danish director Mads Brugger and Swedish private investigator Goran Bjorkdahl, examine the circumstances surrounding the death of UN secretary-general Dag Hammarskjold in a plane crash in 1961. Scores of false starts, dead ends, and elusive interviews later, Brugger and Bjorkdahl begin to sniff out something more monumental than anything they'd initially imagined(n)

programme.title: Tales from the Tour Bus: Rock 'n' Roll on the Road
programme.desc: Rick Wakeman offers a first-hand account of rockers on the road from the late 1950s onwards, recalling the golden age of touring - a secret history of transport cafes, transit vans, B&Bs, sleepless roadies and loved ones left at home. The musician and tour bus aficionado also reflects on the audiences, both good and bad, and the gigs themselves, from the early variety package to the pubs, clubs and stadiums. With contributions by Suzi Quatro and members of Dr Feelgood, the Shadows, the Pretty Things, Fairport Convention, Happy Mondays, Aswad, Girlschool and the Damned(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Timeshift explores the contemporary quest for ancient truths that has arisen out of Dan Brown's best-selling novel challenging the credibility of Christian doctrine. The programme assesses the explosive response to the book from believers and atheists. With contributions by author Richard Leigh and art critic Brian Sewell(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: Historian James Fox explores the country's culture, beginning with the connections between Japanese culture and the natural world and the influence of the country's two major religions of Shintoism and Buddhism(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore describes how the Habsburgs transformed Vienna into a multi-national city of music, culture, art and ideas. Simon explores the Habsburgs' rise to power and discovers more about how Vienna marked Europe's front line in the struggle to defend both Christendom and the Catholic Church from revolutionaries who plotted to destroy them(n)

programme.title: Greg Davies: Looking for Kes
programme.desc: The comedian pays tribute to Ken Loach's 1969 adaptation of Barry Hines' classic novel A Kestrel for a Knave. He travels to Barnsley to meet members of Hines' family, including his brother Richard, who was the inspiration for the book's protagonist Billy Casper. He also talks to Loach and Dai Bradley, who played Billy in the film, and talks to Jarvis Cocker about the character's impact on British pop culture(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Farm Animals
programme.desc: Documentary examining the behaviour of farm animals, testing their intelligence, discovering unlikely relationships and uncovering a side to them that may never have been seen before. The first film focuses on sheep, following 12 weeks in the life of a lamb on a Welsh hill farm, and revealing them to be highly sociable with a remarkable ability to recognise each other and human faces too. Other animals featured include a lonely goose looking for company and a piglet that thinks it is a cow(n)

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britain's most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, in a programme originally shown on its 100th anniversary in 2016. He learns about chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend the UK against terrorist attacks and rogue nations(n)

programme.title: The Trouble With Naipaul
programme.desc: Documentary in which Dr Shahidha Bari examines the cultural legacy of VS Naipaul, exploring how his controversial views on race and history of violence influence how his books are seen through modern eyes. The programme takes a look at the abuse he suffered in childhood, and how it influenced his attitude to the country of his birth, Trinidad and Tobago(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the espionage genre, arguing that the British examples of it allows readers to engage with big questions about the human condition. The conventions of writing about spies are gleaned from Frederick Forsyth, William Boyd, Gerald Seymour, Charles Cumming and former MI5 director general Stella Rimington, and Andrew travels to Berlin in the footsteps of John le Carre, whose experience of witnessing the Wall being erected in 1961 inspired him to write The Spy Who Came in from the Cold(n)

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: The novice crafters are introduced to the art of mosaic making, while origami artist Sam Tsang, demonstrates how to make a family of paper penguins(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.desc: Art historian Professor Richard Clay explores how the idea of utopia has been re-invented by generations of writers and artists. He also examines what utopian visions reveal about our deepest hopes, dreams and fears. Among the contrributors is Star Trek actress Nichelle Nichols(n)

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein explores the transformation of music in the era of Beethoven and Wagner, and in the first episode tells the story of an unrivalled creative outpouring. The presenter reveals how it was possible for composers and performers to become the superstars of their age, no longer the servants of kings and princes(n)

programme.title: The Trouble With Naipaul
programme.desc: Documentary in which Dr Shahidha Bari examines the cultural legacy of VS Naipaul, exploring how his controversial views on race and history of violence influence how his books are seen through modern eyes. The programme takes a look at the abuse he suffered in childhood, and how it influenced his attitude to the country of his birth, Trinidad and Tobago(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. During the Victorian era they gained reputations for criminality, violence and drinking, but was such a reputation deserved? Liz discovers grisly canal crimes, investigates health and welfare on-board working boats, and looks at why canal children were last on the list to be offered safeguards and formal education. She reveals the campaigners who set out to tackle this injustice, including George Smith of Coalville, Leicestershire, and Sister Mary Ward of Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver visits Bronze and Iron Age sites that were important to ancient Britons. At Flag Fen near Peterborough he investigates a vast causeway that has sacred objects placed among its timbers, while at Maiden Castle in Dorset he finds evidence that human sacrifices were conducted to ward off evil spirits. On Anglesey, he learns about the rituals and dark reputation of the druids and how the Romans viewed them as dangerous religious extremists, and at Lullingstone Roman Villa in Kent he examines wall paintings of pagan water deities(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, the graveyard of a Victorian workhouse sheds light on one of the most traumatic periods of modern Irish history, the Great Famine of 1845. Elsewhere, a team from Sheffield University want to understand the lives of people who occupied a village near the famous caves at Cresswell Crags in Nottinghamshire, and near Lincoln, a revisit to an Anglo Saxon site near proves rewarding(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: The return of the comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring sketches, slapstick, songs and stunts, accompanied by a series of special guests. This time, Judge Nutmeg dispenses punishment to an unsuspecting audience member, the Ghost Hunters are on the prowl, and Bob takes viewers back to the 1980s with a special performance(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for barbel in Hay-on-Wye, and sample the great outdoors in spectacular fashion when Mortimer decides to strip in celebration of their catch. Passing a graveyard, they muse about the future and chat to a local vicar about death and their own funerals. To lighten the mood, back in their wooden fishing cabin, Bob promises Paul a very special treat if they are successful with their angling efforts(n)

programme.title: Everyday Miracles: The Genius of Sofas, Stockings and Scanners
programme.desc: Professor Mark Miodownik's investigation into the complexities of modern invention concludes with an insight into how new materials enabled people to become curious about the world around them. He reveals the impact the advent of the bicycle had on the national gene pool, and highights how the concept of levitation provided the spiritually inclined to set out in search of their inner self(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Wally is missing and Elwood City's best detective is called upon. However, that detective is Binky and Fern is determined to prevent him getting the case(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: The team prepares to leave for Regionals. Cassie is made the alternate dancer but still tries to help the team. Giselle drops by on a break from her tour and gives Amanda a special invitation. LaTroy spends time with both Sloane and Amy(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. programme.title: The Man Who Saw Too Much
programme.desc: At 106, Boris Pahor is the oldest living survivor of a Nazi concentration camp. He discusses life and death at Natzweiler, where almost half the prisoners died. Alan Yentob visits Pahor at his home in Trieste, where he talks about his fight against fascism and the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews the leaders of the main parties in the run-up to the General Election(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda backs into another car while taking Ollie to school and drives away, convinced no one has seen. Louise is convinced Lisa is unwell after seeing her medication - but when Keanu tells Sharon, she is left feeling guilty. Ian holds an event to plug his Walford councillor campaign, only for his reputation to be compromised by an unwelcome visitor, and Ben is in high spirits when he manages to help Sonia(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on how China runs its re-education camps, with more than a million people having been locked up in one of the biggest mass detentions in modern history. The investigation uncovers the reality of surveillance and abuse inside hundreds of new detention centres(n)

programme.title: Meat: A Threat to Our Planet?
programme.desc: Animal biologist Liz Bonnin investigates the environmental impact of animals being raised to supply the world's increasing demand for meat, and looks at efforts reduce the effects. She begins her journey in Texas, on a vast intensive farm with 50,000 cows, which emit huge amounts of methane, an incredibly potent greenhouse gas that is contributing to climate change. At a university in California, she hears of the efforts of scientists to better understand a cow's digestion so that they can reduce methane emissions(n)

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler explain and analyse the latest news from the General Election campaign trail(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and comedian David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up Maisie Adam and former Conservative MP Rory Stewart joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests including Hunger Games and Pitch Perfect star Elizabeth Banks, playing Susan Bosley in the new Charlie's Angels movie, which she also wrote and directed. programme.title: River City
programme.desc: A panicked Scarlett worries about the custody battle with Kelly-Marie as the court date looms, while Lou's desperation for quick cash leads her down a dark path, and Sonny nervously faces a tough test to get much-needed help with his arthritis(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Marsha is reunited with Julia on the anniversary of her husband's death, and tries to rebuild their former friendship. However, Benjamin shatters any hope of reconciliation. She is left wondering if her relationship with Ted was worth the price she paid, or if her partner can stand the thought of his ex-wife moving on. Patrick and Della pay their mother a visit, and present her with evidence of Benjamin's indiscretions. She keeps what she knows from him, while plotting to uncover his true motives(n)

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler explain and analyse the latest news from the General Election campaign trail(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian is desperate to deliver the surgery that Bea desires, but Jac is too stressed and refuses to help, forcing him to consider what he is willing to sacrifice to give her what she wants. Dom and Lofty are too busy putting others first to deal with their own problems, and Donna distracts herself from her troubles by focussing on a returning patient. But when she goes missing, Donna has to be brave enough to convince them both to face their fears. Guest starring Lorraine Chase (Emmerdale)(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar report on cosmetic surgery and try to separate fact from fiction. The first episode focuses on nose jobs, and gives four people considering the procedure a glimpse of the process by letting them watch a surgeon operate on a patient. programme.desc: The final part of Barra Best's journey starts at the point where the Lower Bann begins at the northern side of Lough Neagh. programme.title: On the Frontline
programme.desc: During the Troubles nowhere was immune from the violence and fear inflicted on Northern Irish society, not even the schools. programme.desc: Kian is desperate to deliver the surgery that Bea desires, but Jac is too stressed and refuses to help, forcing him to consider what he is willing to sacrifice to give her what she wants. Dom and Lofty are too busy putting others first to deal with their own problems, and Donna distracts herself from her troubles by focussing on a returning patient. But when she goes missing, Donna has to be brave enough to convince them both to face their fears. Guest starring Lorraine Chase (Emmerdale)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Marsha is reunited with Julia on the anniversary of her husband's death, and tries to rebuild their former friendship. However, Benjamin shatters any hope of reconciliation. She is left wondering if her relationship with Ted was worth the price she paid, or if her partner can stand the thought of his ex-wife moving on. Patrick and Della pay their mother a visit, and present her with evidence of Benjamin's indiscretions. She keeps what she knows from him, while plotting to uncover his true motives(n)

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Stephen Dempster investigates allegations that a paedophile network abused boys in the Enniskillen area in the 1980s and '90s, including the story of a man who says he was one of the victims(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar report on cosmetic surgery and try to separate fact from fiction. The first episode focuses on nose jobs, and gives four people considering the procedure a glimpse of the process by letting them watch a surgeon operate on a patient. Each of the viewers is accompanied by a friend or family member, who have their own opinions over whether this is a good idea(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and comedian David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up Maisie Adam and former Conservative MP Rory Stewart joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian hosts an evening of entertainment, recorded in front of an audience at the London Palladium. The first edition features a musical performance by Liam Payne, who performs his latest single, Stack it Up, and survival expert Bear Grylls hands his phone over to the host for Send To All(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case of a poultry farmer who was hanged for the murder of his wife, after her body was found buried under a chicken run(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Tylorstown, South Wales, Rainham, Kent and Crewe in Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holiday makers in Greece. Plus, the children's toys that may contain gender altering chemicals(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: An overturned lorry forces the closure of the road to Edinburgh. Meanwhile, emergency services battle to quell a devastating van fire which refuses to die down(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha and Bear try to convince Emma to lead their 'Smears Without Fears' campaign. Meanwhile, Daniel's therapy session forces him to confront an old demon(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max attempt to find a masked assailant who stole the famous Icon of St Nicholas, but the escape of a person of interest results in a furious Ines removing the pair from the case, causing a rift between the two detectives. When the icon suddenly reappears, Miranda and Max must put their differences aside as they are drawn into a furious cat-and-mouse chase(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A military couple seek a Shropshire home for their £500,000 budget, so Jonnie Irwin goes house hunting with the duo, and also meets a master bike frame builder(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are challenged to come up with a Mughal-inspired design for a tiny garden in Harrow(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a collectable seesaw, a hand stitched bedspread and a gentlemen's shooting stick(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Ulster Unionist Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews the leaders of the main parties in the run-up to the General Election(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Saw Too Much
programme.desc: At 106, Boris Pahor is the oldest living survivor of a Nazi concentration camp. He discusses life and death at Natzweiler, where almost half the prisoners died. Alan Yentob visits Pahor at his home in Trieste, where he talks about his fight against fascism and the Nazis(n)

Showcasing brilliant musicians from Irish and traditional music, TradFest brings us a range of the best talent there is to find, with some of the biggest names in trad and Irish music. Amongst the performers are Eoghan Ó Ceannabháin, An teaghlach tàlantach, Mick, Aoife & Ciara O'Brien and Blazin' Fiddles(n)

programme.title: Leabhraichean-latha a' Chogaidh Mhoir (Great War Diaries)
programme.desc: A Cossack girl experiences a gas attack on the Russian front, an Austrian soldier is transported to Siberia and a German serviceman witnesses the horrors of the Somme(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and topical studio discussion from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers are in Warwick, where David Hakeney assesses a silver box and Fay Rutter is offered a decorative glass bowl and a ruby and diamond ring(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Sanjeev Bhaskar in which players four contestants can accumulate big cash totals by answering questions in a series of rounds that alternate between easy and hard, before one gets to the final stage and the chance to double the prize pot(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn uncovers Al's secret, Jacob is determined to make things right, and Charity gives Tracy some advice(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police come calling for Kel, and Michael begs Grace to give him another chance. Meanwhile, Nick outlines his vision for an all-new Underworld(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: The financial journalist and co-host Angellica Bell returns with more money-saving tips and advice, beginning with up-to-the-minute information on the impending deadline for the Help to Buy ISA scheme. Martin has also been travelling across the country answering the pressing financial concerns of the nation(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A furious Kel locks Bernie inside No 5 with him, while Fiz and Tyrone blame Hope for the fire. Elsewhere, Michael pledges financial support for his daughter(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Tired, hungry, and missing their loved ones, the campmates have to keep one another's morale up as they find out who will face the next Bushtucker Trial. Ant and Dec also present round-up of all the goings-on over the past 24 hours(n)

programme.title: A Very Yorkshire Brothel
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following a year in the life of a mother and daughter who run Sheffield massage parlour City Sauna in the heart of the city's red-light district. programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan splashes out on a new colour television for the family, but has problems disposing of the old one. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector bans all the garage staff from smoking on duty - with dangerous consequences. Starring Reg Varney, Doris Hare, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's plans for a romantic dinner for two are ruined when the Ropers have an argument. Seventies comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is covering her tracks. Jack uncovers a hidden hazard. At Demdyke, Archie fails to cover up for Elsa(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe turns down Lynn's offer. Elsa turns to the law. At the Woolpack, Mitchum turns Shirley's past against her(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis accuses Derek of wallowing in self-pity. Emily and Bernard go on their walking holiday. Ken invites Denise out, but she turns him down(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally starts work at the Kabin. Des gives Jack flowers for Vera from the Hortons for Mother's Day. Maureen asks Maud to accept Reg as she is sick of all the fighting(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Maggie gets the all-clear from the doctor and decides she will return to Bude, forcing PC Penhale to take desperate measures to persuade her to stay in Portwenn. Ruth uncovers a raft of family heirlooms while clearing out a barn at the farm, and the mounting tension between Martin and Louisa peaks when she discovers he has booked a date for James Henry's christening without consulting her(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the fatal shooting of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he walked home from football practice in Croxteth, Liverpool, caught in the crossfire of a feud between rival gangs. Detective Superintendent Dave Kelly is charged with bringing his teenage killer to justice, but though the assailant's identity is well known, finding the evidence to charge him proves to be a far more difficult challenge. Stephen Graham stars(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert struggles to come up with the money to repay loan sharks Alastair and Norman Tonken, who gave him £1,000 to clear his debts. Eleanor falls ill and Martin diagnoses a strangulated hernia, but when the ambulance goes to the wrong village, he has no alternative but to perform the emergency operation himself in his surgery. With Martin Clunes, Ian McNeice and Louise Jameson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Family and friends attend the funeral of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he is laid to rest, while a police raid at the home of Kevin Moody leads to the recovery of the gun used in the schoolboy's murder. Moody must choose between being labelled a grass and risking violent retribution or protecting the killer and facing jail time. But an unexpected result from the forensic tests on the firearm leaves the entire investigation hanging in the balance. Fact-based drama, starring Stephen Graham and Michael Moran(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.desc: Stan and Jack eavesdrop on Blakey's medical - and completely misinterpret its findings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are entranced when they meet Robin's older brother. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A mystery motorcyclist is at large, and a phantom horse haunts the village. Seth makes a macabre discovery(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carollers' peace is shattered by a far from merry ding dong on high, Kathy puts off her day of reckoning, while at the Woolpack, Archie finds his loyalties divided(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg offers to sell the shop flat to Ken. Liz is depressed as she starts back at the Legion. Alex apologises to Tanya about Amsterdam, and assures her she is more than a kept woman(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah Louise comes home, much to the Platts' delight. Ken threatens to withhold his rent from Reg. Maureen worries when Maud doesn't answer her phone(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: When potential witness Claire Olssen is intimidated into changing her statement, the investigation team look for alternative ways to reinforce their case. As Steve returns to work, Kelly is offered a deal that tests his resolve(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

Detective Superintendent Dave Kelly is charged with bringing his teenage killer to justice, but though the assailant's identity is well known, finding the evidence to charge him proves to be a far more difficult challenge. Stephen Graham stars(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan overspends at the dog track and is unable to pay his housekeeping money, so hits on the idea of using the bus to give the senior citizens a day out in return for cash. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A nostalgic Stan remembers his first day at the depot, when he was a conductor and Blakey was a driver. He also recounts the events leading to Arthur and Olive's engagement. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald(n)

When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert struggles to come up with the money to repay loan sharks Alastair and Norman Tonken, who gave him £1,000 to clear his debts. Eleanor falls ill and Martin diagnoses a strangulated hernia, but when the ambulance goes to the wrong village, he has no alternative but to perform the emergency operation himself in his surgery. With Martin Clunes, Ian McNeice and Louise Jameson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Family and friends attend the funeral of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he is laid to rest, while a police raid at the home of Kevin Moody leads to the recovery of the gun used in the schoolboy's murder. Moody must choose between being labelled a grass and risking violent retribution or protecting the killer and facing jail time. But an unexpected result from the forensic tests on the firearm leaves the entire investigation hanging in the balance. Fact-based drama, starring Stephen Graham and Michael Moran(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.desc: Carollers' peace is shattered by a far from merry ding dong on high, Kathy puts off her day of reckoning, while at the Woolpack, Archie finds his loyalties divided(n)

programme.desc: Reg offers to sell the shop flat to Ken. Liz is depressed as she starts back at the Legion. Alex apologises to Tanya about Amsterdam, and assures her she is more than a kept woman(n)

programme.desc: Sarah Louise comes home, much to the Platts' delight. Ken threatens to withhold his rent from Reg. Maureen worries when Maud doesn't answer her phone(n)

programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Spain to investigate how much orange is in orange squash. In snowy Canada, Matt Tebbutt finds out why maple syrup is so expensive, while Kate Quilton reveals the surprising reason why so many of the UK's favourite biscuits have little holes all over them(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elsa and Brian Surridge were on the verge of buying a Spanish property when the 2008 financial crisis scared them off. A decade later they're back and Brian has promised Elsa they won't leave without bagging their dream retirement property on the Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them five very different options for their cash budget of £75,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After years spent dedicated to their demanding careers in nursing and social work, Gillian and Paul Clarke from Stockport have decided it's time to start taking it a little easier and put themselves first. They've got their hearts set on a holiday home on Spain's Costa Cálida where they can boogie their way to retirement. Jonnie Irwin shows the couple five properties for their budget of £45,000(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Vale of Berkeley Guest House in Gloucestershire, where owners Harri and Claire Chiba hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, two guests from rival B&Bs have an issue with the no-frills toilet, and another places a tricky request for 'anaemic toast' at the breakfast table(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Artist Residence in Penzance, Cornwall, where proprietors Justin and Charlie hope to impress their rivals with a pottery class. However, one pair decide their room is too modern for their tastes, a duo are shocked to find hair in a shower filter and one guest seems underwhelmed by breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host are Alec and Craig Coleman, who open the doors to the Edenfield Guest House in Blackpool and invite their fellow B&B owners to rate their establishment. However, on arrival one of the guests and his wife discover stains on the bedspread, while others feel the 'award-winning' food is not up to scratch(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the New Inn, Portland, Dorset, for the fourth day of the competition, where hosts Stacey and Spencer Cullum introduce their guests to a typical island activity - sandcastle building. Meanwhile, Claire Chiba quizzes Stacey about her breakfast hamper and says she is alienating customers, before Harri Chiba tells Justin that his B&B is too 'manufactured'(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Two contestants attempt to explain away their negative feedback, and one contender finds herself unable to hold back her frustration, before the winner of the competition is revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Marlon Williams hosts the first party in Oxford, and hopes his love-island-themed evening will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize at the end of the week. However, after a shambolic start to proceedings, the night ends with the smashing of plates(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Inventor Richard Berrecloth hosts the second dinner party in Oxford, and hopes to impress his guests with home-made food and an unusual form of entertainment. However, Heidi Kelloway and Marlon Williams appear to be more interested in each other than their host's hospitality - while another diner seems rather too keen on sampling the wine(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Victoria Collett hosts the third dinner party in Oxford and begins the day with a hangover and a few culinary disasters. It is not long before her guests start grumbling and Richard Berrecloth criticises her seafood starter, so she pins her hopes on the Caribbean curry being a success(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog trainer James Hearle hosts the fourth dinner party in Oxford, but his four-legged friends threaten to turn the evening into a mess. Tempers also rise between the guests as culinary critic Richard Berrecloth continues shocking the group with his brutal honesty(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Heidi Kelloway hosts the final dinner party in Oxford, determined to lighten the mood of the other diners. However, the evening turns sour when the diners confront the overly critical Richard Berrecloth about his frank comments, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester Zoo's penguins move into a temporary home while a new luxury home is built for them, but the move becomes upsetting when malaria sweeps the colony, with elder Rudd battling to survive against the odds. Meanwhile, the African painted dog pups venture out of their den for the first time, but the runt of the litter Mosi refuses to go out into the cold, and keepers worry she may have issues with her eyesight. Elsewhere, Sumatran orangutan Emma gives birth, and a beetle with a wonky horn has to fight other bachelors in the group to win the attention of a female.(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics battle to keep 48-year-old Paul alive as he suffers a series of heart attacks. Roger's emergency stop halts his day at a scooter rally while, in Cumbria, there's a dramatic mountainside rescue for seriously injured cyclist Nick(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics try to stop hypothermia from setting in when a woman collapses in the snow, while another team treat a pregnant woman whose waters have broken sooner than expected(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. The couple throws a surprise birthday celebration for Angel's mother, where Dick's purpose-built open-fire popcorn maker is put to the test(n)

programme.title: Nazi War Machines: Secrets Uncovered
programme.desc: Historian James Holland examines extraordinary military machines produced under the Third Reich to understand who built them and how they evolved, and why their technically brilliant designs were militarily flawed. He begins with aircraft, learning how superb planes like the Junkers Ju 88 were ruined by Hitler's obsession with dive-bombing(n)

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Russia's War
programme.desc: The story of Germany's invasion of the Soviet Union, revealing the cunning strategy, military technology and defensive structures deployed in this epic struggle. The first edition explores the devastating clash of arms at Sevastopol. one of the world's most heavily defended strongholds(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow couples as they cope with treatment, including Corey, who's 27. He is rushed to St George's after suffering a cardiac arrest during a game of ice hockey, and his wife of seven days, Alice, was watching him play when he collapsed. There's also a profile of John, 55. He fell 10 feet while taking down scaffolding at home, and his spouse Penny talks about how he has supported her through sickness and bereavement during their marriage(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The heli-medics help a man who is fighting for his life following a cardiac arrest, while a tumble on an obstacle course leaves fun-runner Vijitha with a painful open fracture(n)
